full_text:
Thunderstorms, rain, heat wave conditions likely for next 2 days
Share :
Amaravati: The Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority has forecast varied weather conditions across the State over the next two days, including thunderstorms, light to moderate rainfall, and heatwave conditions in some districts.
APSDMA Managing Director Prakhar Jain said that a surface circulation persists over the southwest and adjoining west-central Bay of Bengal, while another circulation extends over Rayalaseema and neighbouring regions. Due to these systems, cloudy weather accompanied by thunderstorms and lightning is likely on Monday and Tuesday. He warned that farmers and cattle-rearers should avoid standing under trees during thunderstorms to prevent lightning-related accidents.
Light to moderate rains with thunderstorms are likely at isolated places in districts including Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, Manyam, Alluri, Polavaram, Anakapalli, Kakinada, Konaseema, East Godavari and West Godavari.
Light rains with lightning are also expected in parts of Eluru, Krishna, NTR, Guntur, Bapatla, Palnadu, Markapuram, Prakasam, Nellore, Annamayya, Chittoor, and Tirupati districts.
Heatwave conditions are likely to prevail in Polavaram district (Chintoor mandal) and parts of NTR district including G Konduru and Ibrahimpatnam mandals.
On Sunday, several areas recorded high temperatures above 40°C. Notable readings include 42.4°C in Veeraghattam (Manyam) and Nellorepalem (Nellore), 42.3°C in Renigunta (Tirupati), 41.8°C in Nandanamarella (Markapuram), and 41.5°C in Ontimitta (Kadapa). Many other regions across the State also recorded temperatures above 40°C, with as many as 66 mandals experiencing intense heat. The APSDMA has advised people to take necessary precautions against heat stress and humid conditions while remaining alert during thunderstorms over the next two days.
